name: environmental_policy_ransomware_incident_kestrel_tru_loy
premise: A Environmental Policy response desk is in a fast-moving incident involving environmental policy ransomware incident kestrel tru loy. Key inputs conflict, but the decision deadline is before the next automated batch runs. Every delay increases harm prevention and accountability. Stakeholders are already framing this as truth vs loyalty under pressure. A live dashboard is public; inconsistencies will trigger immediate investigation. Constraints: Keep public statements strictly factual; label assumptions. Make the intervention reversible and time-boxed with named accountability.

agents.name: Harper Vasquez
agents.identity: I am Harper Vasquez, accountable for a defensible call in Environmental Policy when time is collapsing.
agents.goal: I want to reduce immediate harm without locking in an unjust precedent that later betrays loyalty for truth.

agents.name: Soren Berg
agents.identity: I am Soren Berg, tasked with surfacing second-order harms and who bears the risk in Environmental Policy.
agents.goal: I want guardrails so loyalty isn’t quietly traded away under urgency in the name of truth.

choices: I will take a narrow, time-boxed action with immediate human override and impact checks, accepting slower stabilization to protect loyalty.
I will take a broad, immediate action to stabilize fast and measure impact after deployment, accepting higher risk to loyalty to secure truth under the deadline.
forced_choice.call_to_action: The deadline is before the next automated batch runs. Evidence is partial. Harper Vasquez must decide under truth vs loyalty. What does Harper Vasquez do?
